title: Preeclampsia red flags: when it is an emergency
excerpt: Severe headache, vision changes, and sudden swelling can signal life-threatening preeclampsia — act immediately.
content:
## What is preeclampsia?

High blood pressure with protein in urine or other organ signs, usually after 20 weeks. Can worsen quickly.

## Danger signs

- Severe headache not relieved by usual safe analgesics.
- Blurred vision or flashing lights.
- Sudden facial/hand swelling, upper abdominal pain.
- Decreased fetal movement.

## Nutrition note

Do not extreme salt restriction on your own; follow **medical treatment** including hospital magnesium sulfate when indicated.

## Action

Call emergency services or go to labor ward **immediately** — do not wait for a routine appointment.
